name: Salesforce
what: Salesforce is a leading cloud-based CRM platform offering Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, Marketing Cloud, and more, enabling businesses to manage customer relationships, sales pipelines, and service operations.
auth: OAuth 2.0 (Connected App with client_id/client_secret); also supports username/password OAuth flow and JWT bearer for server-to-server integrations
access_note: Free trial available for most products (Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, etc.) via salesforce.com/crm/free-trial; free developer org available at developer.salesforce.com
api: REST API, SOAP API, Bulk API, Streaming API, Metadata API — all documented at developer.salesforce.com/docs
mcp: Official vendor MCP: Salesforce hosts its own MCP servers (Salesforce Hosted MCP Servers) documented at developer.salesforce.com/docs/platform/hosted-mcp-servers; also an official Salesforce DX MCP Server on GitHub (salesforcecli/mcp)
build: Rich REST/SOAP APIs with OAuth 2.0; official MCP servers for AI agent integration; Apex and Flow for in-platform automation; extensive SDK and tooling ecosystem
blocker: API access requires a Connected App setup within a Salesforce org; some API features (e.g., Bulk API, higher rate limits) may require specific edition/licensing tiers
ev: Multiple developer.salesforce.com docs pages confirming OAuth 2.0 auth; salesforce.com free trial pages confirming self-serve access; official MCP documentation at developer.salesforce.com/docs/platform/hosted-mcp-servers and github.com/salesforcecli/mcp confirming vendor-run MCP

name: HubSpot
what: HubSpot is a CRM and sales platform offering contact, company, deal, and engagement management with a comprehensive REST API for integrations.
auth: OAuth 2.0 (for apps/integrations) and Private App tokens (API keys deprecated); OAuth requires app registration in a HubSpot developer account
access_note: Free developer account available; create a developer test account at developers.hubspot.com to access API keys and OAuth app setup without a paid plan
api: REST API with full CRUD on CRM objects (contacts, companies, deals, engagements, etc.); documented at https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/api/overview
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists; HubSpot launched its own MCP server in public beta (May 2025) at https://developers.hubspot.com/ai-tools/mcp, supporting MCP-compatible AI tools like Cursor and Claude
build: Use OAuth 2.0 or Private App token for auth; REST endpoints for CRM objects; official MCP server available for AI agent integrations; SDKs available for multiple languages
blocker: None significant; developer accounts and test accounts are freely available; OAuth app setup is self-serve
ev: HubSpot developer docs confirm OAuth 2.0 and Private App token auth; official MCP server confirmed at developers.hubspot.com/ai-tools/mcp and changelog entry dated May 6 2025; developer test accounts confirmed in community posts

name: Pipedrive
what: CRM and sales pipeline management platform for SMBs, offering deal tracking, contact management, and sales automation via a RESTful API.
auth: API token (per plan) and OAuth 2.0 (required for Marketplace apps); both supported
access_note: Every Pipedrive plan includes free API access via an API token; OAuth 2.0 app registration is available through the developer portal without gating.
api: REST API v1/v2 fully documented at https://developers.pipedrive.com/docs/api/v1
mcp: Official vendor MCP server launched by Pipedrive (announced via BusinessWire, June 2026), enabling direct connection to AI assistants such as ChatGPT and Claude.
build: Well-documented REST API with OAuth 2.0 and API token auth, webhooks, and a Marketplace for published integrations. Official MCP server now available for AI agent workflows.
blocker: None identified; API is included in all plans and self-serve signup is available.
ev: Pipedrive developer docs (pipedrive.readme.io, developers.pipedrive.com), BusinessWire press release confirming native MCP server launch, pipedrive.com/en/features/crm-api confirming free API access on all plans.

name: Attio
what: AI-native CRM platform for relationship and pipeline management with a REST API, OAuth 2.0, and an official MCP server
auth: API key (workspace-level access token) or OAuth 2.0 bearer token with configurable scopes
access_note: A free trial workspace is self-serve, but a developer/sandbox workspace requires manually contacting Attio via live chat or email (support@attio.com); typically granted within a couple of days
api: REST API documented at https://developers.attio.com; supports OAuth 2.0 and API key auth; scoped access tokens; official SDKs available
mcp: Official vendor MCP server documented at https://docs.attio.com/mcp/overview; community server also exists (kesslerio/attio-mcp-server on GitHub)
build: REST API with OAuth 2.0 or API key; official MCP server for AI agent integration; SDKs available; third-party connectors (Apideck, Stacksync) also available
blocker: Developer workspace requires manual request/approval; no instant self-serve sandbox
ev: docs.attio.com/rest-api/guides/authentication, docs.attio.com/mcp/overview, stacksync.com blog on Attio developer account setup, github.com/kesslerio/attio-mcp-server

name: Twenty
what: Twenty is an open-source CRM platform (self-hosted or cloud-hosted) offering contact management, pipelines, and integrations, positioned as an alternative to Salesforce/Pipedrive.
auth: API key (created in Settings → API & Webhooks) or OAuth 2.1 authorization code flow for external apps acting on behalf of users
access_note: Cloud sign-up available in under a minute with a 30-day trial; self-hosted option also freely available as open-source
api: REST/GraphQL API documented at docs.twenty.com/developers; API keys scoped to roles; OAuth 2.1 supported for third-party integrations
mcp: Community MCP servers exist (jezweb/twenty-mcp on GitHub, igorwarzocha-twenty-mcp-server on LobeHub) but no official vendor-run MCP server found in evidence
build: Strong buildability: open REST/GraphQL API with key and OAuth auth, well-documented developer portal at twenty.com/developers, open-source codebase
blocker: None significant; API is open and self-serve
ev: docs.twenty.com/developers/extend/api, docs.twenty.com/developers/extend/oauth, twenty.com, github.com/jezweb/twenty-mcp, lobehub.com/mcp/igorwarzocha-twenty-mcp-server

name: Podio
what: Podio is a customizable work management and CRM platform offering project management, collaboration, and contact/sales pipeline tools via a REST API.
auth: OAuth 2.0 (authorization code / server-side flow, client credentials, and other grant types supported)
access_note: Free plan available; paid Premium plans exist. API access is self-serve via developer registration at developers.podio.com.
api: REST API with full CRUD coverage of workspaces, items, contacts, users, and more; documented at developers.podio.com
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists — Podio MCP Server documented at docs.sharefile.com (Citrix/Progress-owned Podio docs), connecting AI assistants to Podio data for read/write operations.
build: Register an API key at developers.podio.com, implement OAuth 2.0 flow, call REST endpoints. MCP server available for AI assistant integrations.
blocker: None identified; API and MCP are publicly documented and self-serve.
ev: OAuth docs at developers.podio.com confirm OAuth 2.0 flows; MCP server documented at docs.sharefile.com/en-us/podio/using-podio/general-features/podio-mcp-server.html (official Podio/Progress docs).

name: Zoho CRM
what: Zoho CRM is a cloud-based customer relationship management platform for sales, marketing, and support automation, offering extensive developer APIs and a low-code/pro-code developer platform.
auth: OAuth 2.0 (short-lived access tokens + refresh tokens); API key authentication also supported for serverless functions
access_note: Free 15-day trial available with no gating; sign-up at zoho.com/crm/signup.html; free tier for up to 3 users on paid plans
api: REST API v6/v8 fully documented at https://www.zoho.com/crm/developer/docs/api/v6/ with OAuth 2.0; comprehensive developer platform including webhooks, SDKs, and functions
mcp: Community/third-party MCP servers exist (LokiMCPUniverse, junnaisystems on GitHub, listed on mcp.aibase.com and mcpmarket.com) but no official vendor-run MCP server confirmed in evidence
build: Strong REST API with OAuth 2.0, SDKs (Python, Java, PHP, Node.js, etc.), webhooks, serverless functions, and Zoho Developer Console for app registration
blocker: None significant; API access requires registering an OAuth app in Zoho Developer Console, which is self-serve
ev: Official Zoho docs confirm OAuth 2.0 (v8 overview page), API key auth for functions, self-serve signup page, and developer platform page; MCP evidence is community-only (GitHub repos, third-party aggregators)

name: Close
what: Close is a CRM and sales platform with a full REST API supporting lead, contact, opportunity, activity, and pipeline management, targeted at sales teams.
auth: API key (HTTP Basic) and OAuth 2.0 (authorization code flow with refresh token rotation)
access_note: Developers can generate API keys directly from their Close account settings; OAuth apps require registering a developer application via the Close developer platform, which appears self-serve based on the developer portal.
api: Full REST API documented at developer.close.com with interactive docs, schemas, and examples.
mcp: none known (unverified)
build: Yes — Close Developer Platform explicitly supports customers automating sales processes and partners building integrations, including AI agent use cases.
blocker: None identified; API access is tied to having a Close subscription (paid CRM product), but API keys are self-provisioned within the account.
ev: developer.close.com landing page confirms developer platform; help.close.com/docs/api-keys-oauth and developer.close.com/api/overview/oauth-authentication confirm both auth methods.

name: Copper
what: Copper is a CRM platform built for Google Workspace, offering contact, lead, and deal management with a developer API for integrations.
auth: API Key (for individual users) and OAuth 2.0 (for partner/app integrations); OAuth requires registering your application with Copper first
access_note: API Key access appears self-serve via account settings; OAuth 2.0 requires contacting Copper to register your application before use
api: REST API documented at developer.copper.com; supports API keys and OAuth 2.0; rate limits documented; supports scopes for OAuth
mcp: none known (unverified)
build: Yes — REST API with API key or OAuth 2.0 auth, developer docs at developer.copper.com, client libraries available
blocker: OAuth 2.0 integration requires manual application registration with Copper (not fully self-serve); API key access should be straightforward for individual use
ev: developer.copper.com official docs confirm dual auth modes; Nango docs corroborate OAuth registration requirement via contacting Copper

name: DealCloud
what: DealCloud is a CRM and deal management platform for private equity, investment banking, and M&A advisory professionals, built on Azure and owned by Intapp.
auth: API key (self-serve via profile settings) and OAuth Bearer token; SDK (Python) supports JSON config-based credential initialization.
access_note: Product requires a demo/sales engagement per Intapp website; API keys are enabled per user within an existing subscription. No self-serve sign-up evidence found.
api: REST API v4 (documented at api.docs.dealcloud.com); Python SDK available; supports per-user delegation via AS-USER header.
mcp: Official vendor MCP server in client preview — snippet from api.docs.dealcloud.com states '🚀 New: DealCloud MCP Server — client preview sta[rted]', indicating a vendor-run MCP server currently in preview.
build: Well-documented REST API with versioned endpoints, API key + OAuth auth, Python SDK, and an emerging official MCP server — strong foundation for integration.
blocker: Access requires an existing DealCloud/Intapp subscription (enterprise sales process); no public free tier or self-serve trial evident.
ev: api.docs.dealcloud.com docs confirm API key flow, OAuth Bearer tokens, REST v4 endpoints, Python SDK, and vendor MCP server preview announcement.

name: Zendesk
what: Customer support and helpdesk platform offering ticketing, live chat, knowledge base, and CRM (Sell) tools via a comprehensive REST API.
auth: OAuth 2.0 (Authorization Code, Implicit, Resource Owner Password grants) and API tokens; OAuth tokens created/managed via API or Admin Center; API tokens created via Admin Center.
access_note: 14-day free trial available without contacting sales; paid plans start at $19/month. Developer/sponsored accounts also available for partners.
api: Full REST API documented at https://developer.zendesk.com/api-reference covering Support, Chat, Sell/CRM, Guide, and Talk products.
mcp: Official Zendesk MCP support exists (early access program per Zendesk support docs and TechRadar); community/third-party servers also available (scottolsen, mattcoatsworth on GitHub). Vendor MCP is in EAP, not yet GA.
build: Integrate via REST API with OAuth or API token auth; use Zendesk Marketplace (1,200+ integrations); webhooks and event triggers available; SDKs exist for mobile. MCP integration available in EAP for AI agent workflows.
blocker: MCP support is in Early Access Program (EAP) only—requires opt-in activation. Full feature access requires paid plan.
ev: Developer docs confirm OAuth and API token auth; pricing page confirms self-serve trial; Zendesk support article confirms MCP EAP; TechRadar confirms official Zendesk MCP adoption.

name: Intercom
what: Customer messaging and helpdesk platform with AI agent (Fin), supporting conversations, contacts, tickets, and workflows for customer service.
auth: OAuth 2.0 (for integrations accessing other accounts) or API key as Bearer token (for own account/Fin Agent API). Both documented on developers.intercom.com.
access_note: Free trial available directly on intercom.com; Developer Hub accessible without sales contact. OAuth and API key generation self-serve via Developer Hub.
api: Yes — REST API documented at developers.intercom.com, covering conversations, contacts, companies, tickets, and more. SDKs available for Web, iOS, Android.
mcp: Official vendor MCP server — Intercom hosts and documents their own MCP server at developers.intercom.com/docs/guides/mcp, enabling AI agents to access Intercom conversations, contacts, and data. Currently limited to US-hosted workspaces.
build: Strong integration target: REST API, OAuth 2.0, API keys, webhooks, SDKs (Web/iOS/Android), official MCP server, and a Developer Hub with integration guides.
blocker: MCP server currently only supported in US-hosted workspaces, which may limit availability for non-US customers.
ev: developers.intercom.com/docs/guides/mcp (official MCP docs), developers.intercom.com/docs/build-an-integration/learn-more/authentication (OAuth + API key docs), developers.intercom.com/docs/guides/fin-agent-api/setup (API key generation).

name: Freshdesk
what: Customer support helpdesk platform with ticketing, knowledge base, and agent collaboration tools
auth: API key (Basic auth using API key as username); OAuth not supported for direct API access
access_note: Free trial available with self-serve signup; API key accessible from profile settings on any paid or trial account
api: REST API v2 at https://developers.freshdesk.com/api; well-documented with full CRUD on tickets, contacts, agents, companies, conversations
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists (Freshworks-run, currently EAP) per support.freshdesk.com/support/solutions/articles/50000012670; enables AI tools like Cursor and Claude to connect to Freshdesk
build: Straightforward integration: obtain API key from profile, use Basic auth (key:X), REST calls. Official MCP server available in EAP for AI agent workflows.
blocker: MCP integration is in Early Access Program (EAP) — may require opt-in or approval. No OAuth for API; only static API keys.
ev: Official API docs at developers.freshdesk.com/api confirmed; auth method confirmed via community forum and eesel.ai blog; MCP confirmed via official Freshdesk support article (EAP); free trial/self-serve confirmed via freshworks.com/freshdesk/pricing/free

name: Front
what: Front is a customer communication platform (shared inbox, help desk) with a Core API giving full programmatic access to conversations, contacts, comments, and more.
auth: API tokens (for internal/personal integrations) or OAuth 2.0 (required for partner/multi-tenant integrations); both use Bearer token in Authorization header.
access_note: Free developer account available at dev.frontapp.com with access to a sandbox environment (limited to 3 teammates). Paid plans purchasable without sales contact.
api: REST Core API at dev.frontapp.com; comprehensive CRUD over all Front entities (conversations, contacts, inboxes, comments, tags, etc.).
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists (beta), announced on Front's community forum, supporting Claude, Cursor, Windsurf and other AI tools.
build: Straightforward to build on: well-documented REST API, self-serve developer accounts, API tokens for quick internal use, OAuth for multi-tenant apps.
blocker: Partners building Core API integrations that write on behalf of customers must use OAuth and may need an exception from Front for API-token-only approaches.
ev: dev.frontapp.com docs (API tokens, OAuth, introduction pages); community.front.com MCP beta announcement; getmacha.com auth overview; xpay.sh pricing note.

name: Pylon
what: B2B customer support & helpdesk platform focused on Slack-based customer interactions, ticketing, and issue tracking
auth: Bearer token (API key) via Team Settings; OAuth flows also supported per Nango/Truto integration docs
access_note: API access requires visiting API Settings in Team Settings and contacting Pylon support staff per their developer hub
api: REST API at api.usepylon.com; documented at docs.usepylon.com/pylon-docs/developer/api
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists; documented at docs.usepylon.com/pylon-docs/integrations/pylon-mcp and open-source reference impl at github.com/JustinBeckwith/pylon-mcp
build: REST API with Bearer auth; official MCP server for AI agent integration; third-party unified API support via Unified.to, Truto, Nango
blocker: API access is gated — must contact Pylon support to enable; no fully self-serve API key provisioning documented
ev: Pylon developer hub explicitly states 'contact our support staff' for API access; docs.usepylon.com confirms Bearer auth; official MCP page confirmed at docs.usepylon.com/pylon-docs/integrations/pylon-mcp

name: LiveAgent
what: Help desk and customer support platform with live chat, ticketing, call center, and knowledge base features.
auth: API key (per-account, set in configuration header as 'apikey')
access_note: Free trial available; paid plans start at $15/agent/month. Signup is self-serve via liveagent.com/pricing.
api: REST API with full API reference documented at support.liveagent.com; official Python SDK available on GitHub (QualityUnit/LiveAgent-api-python).
mcp: none known (unverified) — no official LiveAgent MCP server found in evidence; MCP snippets in results relate to LiveKit and other unrelated products.
build: Yes — REST API + API key auth; official Python client library; supports custom integrations and automation.
blocker: None identified. API key is self-provisioned within account settings.
ev: GitHub README for official Python SDK shows API key auth pattern; support.liveagent.com REST API docs confirm API key mechanism; pricing page confirms self-serve access.

name: Plain
what: Plain is an API-first customer support / helpdesk platform designed for B2B SaaS, offering ticketing, AI-assisted support, and deep extensibility via APIs.
auth: OAuth 2.0 with PKCE (Plain MCP server); API key/Bearer for REST/GraphQL API
access_note: Plain's site directs to 'Book a demo' for sign-up, suggesting sales-assisted onboarding; no self-serve free trial evident from evidence.
api: Yes — documented REST/GraphQL API at https://www.plain.com/docs/api-reference
mcp: Official Plain MCP server exists (mcp.plain.com) — 30 tools across threads, customers, tenants, help center; OAuth-based, read+write. Confirmed via Plain's own help center and changelog (help.plain.com/article/mcp-server, plain.com/changelog/plain-mcp), not just the originally-cited blog post.
build: Feasible; Plain explicitly positions itself as API-first with public API reference docs, enabling integrations for ticket management, customer data, and AI support workflows.
blocker: Access may require a sales conversation / demo before API credentials are provisioned; no confirmed self-serve sandbox.
ev: Plain's own blog and site reference API-first design and demo-booking CTA; hint URL confirms public API reference exists; no Composio toolkit match; search snippets do not contain Plain-specific auth or MCP details.
_pre_audit_mcp: Official Plain MCP server exists, using OAuth 2.0 with PKCE (https://www.plain.com/blog/mcp-customer-support-2026)
audit_note: Original agent run asserted this MCP claim as HIGH/verified-doc while its own evidence field admitted no supporting snippet was retrieved. Claim was independently re-verified as TRUE via direct site check + fresh search, but flagged as a process failure: confidence label did not match its own evidence trail.

name: Help Scout
what: Help Scout is an all-in-one customer support platform offering shared inboxes, live chat, a knowledge base (Docs), and reporting for growing businesses.
auth: OAuth 2.0 (Authorization Code and Client Credentials flows) for the Mailbox API; API key per user for the Docs API
access_note: Free 15-day trial (Standard/Plus) with no credit card required; Pro plan trial requires sales contact. API access available on all paid plans; SCIM requires Pro.
api: Yes — REST API (Mailbox API and Docs API) documented at https://developer.helpscout.com; SCIM provisioning available on Pro plan.
mcp: Community/third-party only — GravityKit open-source MCP server on GitHub (github.com/GravityKit/help-scout-mcp), MCPMarket listing, and StackOne commercial connector. No official vendor-run MCP server found.
build: Use OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials (server-to-server) or Authorization Code flow; base URL https://api.helpscout.net/v2/; Docs API uses per-user API key. Well-documented REST endpoints for conversations, mailboxes, customers, tags, workflows, and Docs content.
blocker: SCIM and SSO require Pro plan (sales-assisted trial). No other significant blockers for standard API use.
ev: Official auth docs at developer.helpscout.com confirm OAuth 2.0; Stitchflow confirms SCIM on Pro; Help Scout pricing page confirms self-serve trial with no credit card.

name: Gorgias
what: Customer support helpdesk platform for ecommerce, managing tickets, live chat, macros, rules, and customer data
auth: API key (HTTP Basic Auth with email+key) for private/server-to-server integrations; OAuth2 for public integrations
access_note: API keys generated directly from account settings; no approval gate evident
api: REST API documented at https://developers.gorgias.com; supports tickets, messages, customers, macros, rules, integrations, widgets
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists; documented at https://docs.gorgias.com/en-US/connect-your-ai-assistant-to-the-gorgias-mcp-6310546 with 114 tools; also available via Composio
build: Strong integration target: well-documented REST API, self-serve keys, official MCP, available on Make and Prismatic; good for ticket management, customer data sync, AI-driven support automation
blocker: None significant; rate limits and field changes should be verified in live docs per vendor notice
ev: Multiple independent sources confirm API auth methods; official Gorgias docs confirm MCP server existence

name: Gladly
what: Gladly is an AI-powered customer support platform offering helpdesk, live chat, email, SMS, and voice support with a REST API for integrations.
auth: API key (token-based auth); tokens are generated in the Gladly admin and used as HTTP Basic Auth credentials (username + API token as password).
access_note: Gladly is a paid SaaS platform sold via sales/demo request. A 30-day free trial exists via the Shopify App Store for that integration, but direct API access requires an active Gladly account obtained through sales.
api: Yes — REST API documented at https://developer.gladly.com/rest with webhooks, contacts, messaging, and more.
mcp: none known (unverified)
build: Yes — REST API with API key auth is available. Third-party integration platforms (Truto, Merge) also offer pre-built connectors.
blocker: Requires a Gladly account (sales-gated); no self-serve API sandbox publicly available.
ev: developer.gladly.com REST API docs confirm token-based auth and endpoint coverage; help.gladly.com confirms token generation flow; contact page confirms sales-only onboarding; no official MCP server found in any evidence.

name: Slack
what: Slack is a cloud-based team messaging and collaboration platform offering channels, direct messaging, file sharing, and app integrations.
auth: OAuth 2.0 authorization code grant flow; also supports bot tokens and user tokens with scoped permissions via OAuth scopes.
access_note: Free tier available; paid plans (Pro, Business+, Enterprise+) with self-serve signup at slack.com/pricing. Enterprise+ requires contacting sales.
api: REST-based Web API at api.slack.com/web; supports methods like oauth.v2.access; comprehensive developer docs at docs.slack.dev.
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists; Slack publishes and documents their own MCP server at docs.slack.dev/ai/slack-mcp-server and slack.com/help, enabling AI agents to connect to Slack via MCP.
build: Slack Developer Docs (docs.slack.dev), API reference, OAuth guide at api.slack.com; SDKs available via Slack GitHub (slackhq).
blocker: Enterprise Grid / Enterprise+ tier requires sales contact. App marketplace integrations may have their own terms. Slack API Terms of Service apply to all API usage.
ev: Official Slack developer docs (docs.slack.dev/authentication, docs.slack.dev/ai/slack-mcp-server), Slack help article on MCP (slack.com/help), pricing page (slack.com/pricing), GitHub oauth docs (slackhq/slack-api-docs).

name: Twilio
what: Cloud communications platform providing APIs for SMS, voice, video, email, and authentication (OTP/Verify). Enables developers to build programmable messaging and calling into applications.
auth: HTTP Basic Auth using API Key + API Key Secret (recommended); OAuth 2.0 client credentials grant also supported via OAuth apps
access_note: Sign up via Twilio Console, free trial available, API keys generated immediately in Console or via API
api: Comprehensive REST API covering messaging, voice, video, verify, IAM, and more; documented at https://www.twilio.com/docs/usage/api
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists; maintained by twilio-labs, exposes full Twilio public API via MCP. Announced on Twilio blog and listed at mcpservers.org/servers/twilio-labs/mcp
build: Strong integration candidate: self-serve REST API with API key auth, official MCP server, extensive docs, SDKs in multiple languages, webhooks for inbound events
blocker: None significant; trial accounts have sandbox restrictions (e.g., verified numbers only for SMS) until account is upgraded with billing
ev: Multiple official Twilio docs pages confirming API key/Basic Auth and OAuth support; official Twilio blog posts explicitly announcing Twilio MCP server (twilio-labs/mcp); mcpservers.org listing confirming official status

name: Zoho Cliq
what: Zoho Cliq is a team messaging and collaboration platform with channels, direct messaging, bots, and integrations, offering a REST API v3 for developers.
auth: OAuth 2.0 (client ID + client secret registered via Zoho API Console; access and refresh tokens)
access_note: Developers can register applications and obtain API credentials via the Zoho API Console without a gated approval process.
api: REST API v3 (https://www.zoho.com/cliq/help/restapi/v3/introduction); endpoints for messaging, settings, channels, shortcuts, DND, user preferences; self-serve registration.
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists (https://www.zoho.com/cliq/help/platform/zoho-cliq-mcp.html and https://www.zoho.com/cliq/mcp-server.html); supports Cursor, Windsurf, Claude, VS Code and more.
build: Yes – full REST API, webhooks/bots via Cliq Developer Platform, official MCP server, and broad third-party integration ecosystem.
blocker: None identified; API and MCP are publicly documented and self-serve.
ev: Zoho's own docs confirm OAuth 2.0 setup, REST API v3, and an official MCP server page at zoho.com/cliq/mcp-server.html and help/platform/zoho-cliq-mcp.html.

name: Lark (Larksuite)
what: Lark (Larksuite) is an all-in-one collaboration platform combining messaging, docs, calendar, tasks, and CRM, with a comprehensive open API platform for building integrations and bots.
auth: OAuth 2.0 (authorization code flow for user_access_token + refresh_token) and app-level tenant_access_token via App ID + App Secret
access_note: Developers can self-register on open.larksuite.com, create an app, and obtain credentials immediately without manual approval gating.
api: Yes — extensive REST Server API covering messaging, docs, calendar, auth, events, and more; documented at open.larksuite.com/document
mcp: Official vendor MCP server: github.com/larksuite/lark-openapi-mcp (maintained by larksuite org, wraps full OpenAPI surface as MCP tools)
build: Yes — Lark Open Platform supports custom apps, bots, webhooks, and third-party integrations via documented APIs and SDKs.
blocker: None significant; app review may be required before publishing to Lark Marketplace, but development/testing is self-serve.
ev: open.larksuite.com docs confirm OAuth flows and Server API list; GitHub repo larksuite/lark-openapi-mcp confirms official vendor MCP; mcp.so and lobehub entries corroborate official MCP status.

name: Pumble
what: Team messaging and collaboration platform with channels, direct messages, and video conferencing
auth: API key (self-generated via Pumble app install) and OAuth2 (for app integrations via pumble-sdk)
access_note: Free plan available; 7-day free trial for paid features; API key generation available to all workspace users after installing API addon
api: REST API documented at pumble.com/developers/api; pumble-sdk npm package available; supports webhooks/triggers
mcp: Official vendor MCP server confirmed (pumble.com/help/integrations/automation-workflow-integrations/how-to-use-the-pumble-mcp-server); also community MCP (pumble-mcp on mcpmarket/lobehub)
build: REST API + API keys + OAuth2 app framework (pumble-sdk); MCP server for AI agent integration; also available via MindCloud connector
blocker: None identified; API access is self-serve
ev: pumble.com/help/integrations/automation-workflow-integrations/api-keys-integration confirms self-serve API key generation; pumble.com/help/integrations/automation-workflow-integrations/how-to-use-the-pumble-mcp-server confirms official MCP; npmjs.com/package/pumble-sdk confirms OAuth2 SDK

name: Discord
what: Discord is a communication platform offering text, voice, and video chat, with a robust API for building bots, apps, and integrations via the Discord Developer Portal.
auth: OAuth2 (for user authorization) and Bot Token (for bot/application authentication); both are supported and documented officially.
access_note: Developers can self-register an application and obtain credentials immediately via the Discord Developer Portal with no approval gating for standard API access.
api: Extensive REST API and Gateway (WebSocket) API; well-documented at https://docs.discord.com/developers/reference. Supports bots, OAuth2 apps, activities, and more.
mcp: Community/unofficial MCP servers exist (e.g., exilproductions/discord-mcp, SaseQ/discord-mcp, v-3/discordmcp on GitHub) but no official vendor-run MCP server found in evidence.
build: Yes — full developer platform with REST API, WebSocket Gateway, OAuth2, SDKs, and extensive official documentation at https://discord.com/developers/docs.
blocker: None for standard use. Rate limits apply. Some features (e.g., privileged intents like message content) require verification/approval for larger bots.
ev: Multiple official Discord Developer Portal doc pages confirm self-serve registration, OAuth2, and bot token auth. GitHub repo discord/discord-api-docs confirms official docs. Community MCP servers found but no official Discord MCP.

name: Telegram
what: Telegram Bot API and MTProto API for building bots, sending messages, and integrating with Telegram's messaging platform
auth: Bot Token (via BotFather) for Bot API; OAuth/phone-based auth for MTProto/user accounts
access_note: Free and self-serve; obtain a bot token from BotFather instantly at t.me/BotFather; MTProto API requires app registration at my.telegram.org
api: https://core.telegram.org/bots/api
mcp: community (multiple third-party MCP servers exist, e.g. BrainDAO/mcp-telegram, dryeab/mcp-telegram, tacticlaunch/mcp-telegram; no official Telegram-run MCP server found)
build: REST-style Bot API (HTTPS polling or webhooks); MTProto binary protocol for user-level access; official SDKs in multiple languages
blocker: None for Bot API; MTProto app registration may require phone verification and manual review for high-volume usage
ev: Official Bot API docs at core.telegram.org/bots/api confirmed; self-serve bot token creation via BotFather confirmed; multiple community MCP servers confirmed; no official Telegram MCP server found in evidence

name: WhatsApp Business
what: WhatsApp Business Platform (Cloud API) — Meta's official API for businesses to send/receive WhatsApp messages at scale, including templates, webhooks, and two-way messaging.
auth: OAuth 2.0 and/or system user access tokens (Meta App credentials); business verification required before production access
access_note: Requires Meta business verification and app review before full production access; sandbox/test numbers available during development via Meta for Developers dashboard
api: REST (WhatsApp Business Cloud API hosted by Meta); also on-premise API option via BSPs
mcp: No official Meta/WhatsApp vendor MCP server; community/third-party MCP servers exist (e.g., Wassenger, Blueticks, lharries/whatsapp-mcp on GitHub) but none are official Meta-run
build: Yes — REST API with webhooks, well-documented at developers.facebook.com/docs/whatsapp; SDKs available via Meta and BSPs; integrations via n8n, Nango, UiPath, Twilio, etc.
blocker: Business verification and Meta app approval gate production use; template messages require pre-approval; no official MCP from Meta
ev: Meta developer docs (hint URL), n8n credential docs, Nango integration docs, UiPath integration docs confirming OAuth 2.0 auth flow, multiple BSP/third-party MCP server mentions confirming no official Meta MCP

name: Aircall
what: Cloud-based phone system and call center platform for sales and support teams, offering virtual numbers, CRM integrations, calls, SMS, contacts, tags, webhooks, and AI summaries via a public REST API.
auth: HTTP Basic Auth (api_id as username, api_token as password); OAuth2 Bearer token available for multi-customer integrations.
access_note: Developer docs and API keys available directly at developer.aircall.io; free trial available on aircall.io.
api: Public REST API at https://developer.aircall.io/api-references; HTTPS only; rate limits apply; endpoints for users, calls, numbers, contacts, tags, webhooks, and Aircall AI.
mcp: Community/third-party only. A community MCP server (themobilefirstco-aircall-mcp-server, MIT licensed, 73 tools) exists on npm and is listed on LobeHub. Zapier and viaSocket also offer Aircall MCP integrations. No official vendor-run MCP server found in evidence.
build: Build integrations using the public REST API with Basic Auth or OAuth2; webhooks available for real-time events; Aircall Labs offers experimental apps for active customers.
blocker: None identified; API is self-serve with public documentation.
ev: developer.aircall.io official docs confirmed; Basic Auth and OAuth2 confirmed via dlthub and developer.aircall.io tutorials; community MCP confirmed via LobeHub snippet.

name: Vonage
what: Communications APIs platform providing SMS, voice, video, messaging (WhatsApp, RCS), and verification services for developers
auth: API Key + Secret (Basic Auth for testing) and JWT Bearer tokens for production; CAMARA OAuth2 for network APIs
access_note: Free credit available to start developing immediately; no gating mentioned
api: REST APIs documented at developer.vonage.com; supports webhooks/callbacks; SDKs available via Vonage CLI
mcp: Official Vonage MCP Server exists (vendor-run); documented at developer.vonage.com/en/mcp-server/overview — includes both a Documentation MCP and a Telephony MCP Server exposing Vonage APIs to AI agents/LLMs via Model Context Protocol
build: Build communications features (SMS, voice calls, video, WhatsApp, RCS, verification) into apps; can integrate with AI agents via MCP; contact center and AI Studio solutions also available
blocker: None identified; self-serve with free trial credit
ev: developer.vonage.com authentication docs confirm API Key/JWT auth; vonage.com/communications-apis confirms self-serve free credit; developer.vonage.com/en/mcp-server/overview and /technical-details confirm official vendor MCP server

name: Google Ads
what: Google Ads API — programmatic interface for creating, managing, and reporting on Google Ads campaigns and accounts at scale.
auth: OAuth 2.0 (web/desktop/service account flows) plus a developer token required in every request header; Manager Account login-customer-id header also required for MCC access.
access_note: Requires applying for and receiving a developer token from a Google Ads Manager Account; Basic access granted quickly but Standard access requires additional application/review. Test accounts available for development.
api: REST and gRPC; versioned (e.g., v17/v18); full CRUD plus reporting via GAQL. Docs: https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/docs/start
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists — Google released an official Google Ads MCP Server (announced via YouTube/Google for Developers), enabling LLM access to Google Ads API via Model Context Protocol.
build: Well-documented REST/gRPC API with client libraries (Python, Java, PHP, Ruby, .NET, Perl). OAuth2 setup is the main integration hurdle alongside developer token approval.
blocker: Developer token gating: must have a Google Ads Manager Account and apply for a token; Standard access tier requires approval review which can add friction for new integrators.
ev: Official docs at developers.google.com/google-ads/api/docs/start; GitHub auth guide; YouTube official MCP announcement video; multiple third-party integration references confirming OAuth2 + developer token requirement.

name: Meta Ads
what: Meta's Marketing API (formerly Facebook Ads API) for programmatic management of ad campaigns, creatives, budgets, targeting, and reporting across Facebook/Instagram/Meta platforms.
auth: OAuth 2.0 (access tokens with scopes such as ads_read, ads_management); app must be registered in Meta Business Manager; production use requires formal App Review by Meta.
access_note: Free API access in tiered system (Development, Standard, Advanced); Development tier is self-serve but production/Standard+ requires Meta App Review approval. No per-call fees from Meta.
api: REST (Graph API / Marketing API), current version v24.0; https://developers.facebook.com/docs/marketing-apis
mcp: Community MCP by pipeboard-co (github.com/pipeboard-co/meta-ads-mcp, 187 stars) is the most prominent; a YouTube video claims it is 'official Meta' but no direct Meta developer docs confirm vendor-run MCP — treat as community/third-party.
build: Full REST API with extensive docs; SDKs available (Python, PHP, Java, Ruby, Node via Meta Business SDK); token lifecycle management, rate limits, and quarterly schema changes add integration complexity.
blocker: App Review required for production access beyond Development tier; OAuth token management (short-lived vs long-lived tokens); strict rate limits per ad account; quarterly API version deprecations.
ev: Strong — official Meta developer docs linked in hint; multiple third-party integration guides confirm auth, tiers, and scopes; MCP evidence is community-sourced only.

name: LinkedIn Ads
what: LinkedIn Ads (LinkedIn Marketing API) — programmatic access to ad campaign management, reporting, lead gen forms, conversions, and audience targeting on LinkedIn
auth: OAuth 2.0 (three-legged); Client ID + Client Secret registered via LinkedIn Developer Portal; scopes such as rw_ads and r_ads_reporting required; production access requires LinkedIn review/approval
access_note: Self-serve to register app and build/test; production access to Marketing API products (Advertising, Conversions, Lead Sync, etc.) requires submitting for LinkedIn review via the Products tab in the Developer Portal — not immediately live
api: REST API documented at https://learn.microsoft.com/linkedin/marketing; endpoints cover campaign management, reporting, lead gen, conversions, and more
mcp: Community/third-party only — multiple community MCP servers exist (e.g., radiateb2b/mcp-linkedinads on GitHub, danielpopamd/linkedin-ads-mcp on mcpservers.org); no official LinkedIn/Microsoft-run MCP server found in evidence
build: Register a LinkedIn Developer app, obtain Client ID/Secret, request Marketing API product access (reviewed), implement OAuth 2.0 three-legged flow, then call Marketing API endpoints for campaigns, reporting, etc.
blocker: Production access is gated behind LinkedIn's review process; apps stay in a sandbox/test mode until approved; review timelines and approval criteria are not fully transparent
ev: Stitchflow auth guide (OAuth 2.0 + scopes), YouTube/UnifiedAPI noting review process, Elfsight table showing 'self-serve to build; reviewed for production', Microsoft Learn OAuth docs, community MCP repos on GitHub and mcpservers.org

name: GoHighLevel
what: GoHighLevel (HighLevel) is an all-in-one CRM and marketing automation platform for agencies, offering tools for contacts, pipelines, calendars, reputation management, and workflow automation.
auth: OAuth 2.0 (for Marketplace/public apps) or Private Integration Token/API Key (for private integrations); tiered API access with Agency API Keys on higher plans
access_note: 14-day free trial available; API access included with subscription; Advanced API (OAuth 2.0 full scope) requires higher-tier plan
api: Yes — REST API documented at https://highlevel.stoplight.io and https://marketplace.gohighlevel.com/docs; v1 and v2 endpoints; rate limits apply by plan tier
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists — HighLevel publishes their own LeadConnector MCP Server documented at https://marketplace.gohighlevel.com/docs/other/mcp and supported at https://help.gohighlevel.com/support/solutions/articles/155000007981
build: Developers can build Marketplace apps (OAuth 2.0, publicly listed) or private integrations (API key); webhooks supported; interactive API docs available; community and third-party MCP servers also exist
blocker: Full OAuth 2.0 / Advanced API access requires a higher-tier paid plan; Agency API Keys gated behind plan level
ev: Official developer portal (developers.gohighlevel.com), API docs (marketplace.gohighlevel.com/docs), official MCP docs (marketplace.gohighlevel.com/docs/other/mcp), support article on MCP, pricing page confirming trial and enterprise tiers

name: Mailchimp
what: Email and marketing automation platform with REST API for managing audiences, campaigns, automations, templates, and transactional email.
auth: API key (HTTP Basic or Bearer) or OAuth 2.0; OAuth 2.0 required for Integration Partner Program / Marketplace listing.
access_note: Free tier available; paid plans by contact list size; custom/enterprise pricing via sales for large lists.
api: REST API (Marketing API v3); full reference at https://mailchimp.com/developer/marketing/api; also Transactional (Mandrill) API available separately.
mcp: none official; community/third-party MCP servers exist (StackOne, Apify, GitHub community repos) but no vendor-run official MCP server found in evidence.
build: Well-documented public API, OAuth 2.0 + API key auth, comprehensive endpoints for audiences, campaigns, automations, reports. Good candidate for integration builds.
blocker: None significant; OAuth 2.0 required only if seeking Marketplace listing. API key sufficient for private integrations.
ev: Mailchimp developer docs confirm API key + OAuth 2.0 auth, self-serve access with free tier, and full API reference publicly available.

name: Klaviyo
what: Email/SMS marketing automation and CRM platform for e-commerce brands, offering list management, flows, campaigns, analytics, and segmentation.
auth: Private API key (header: Authorization: Klaviyo-API-Key) for direct API access; OAuth 2.0 (PKCE, authorization_code flow) for tech partner/third-party app integrations.
access_note: Free plan available with no credit card required; paid tiers unlock higher send volumes and support channels. API keys accessible immediately after signup.
api: REST API at developers.klaviyo.com; versioned JSON:API spec; supports profiles, lists, segments, campaigns, flows, events, metrics, and more.
mcp: Official vendor MCP server (generally available as of August 2025); documented at developers.klaviyo.com/en/docs/klaviyo_mcp_server; connects AI clients (Claude, Cursor) to Klaviyo data and tools.
build: Well-documented REST API with private key or OAuth auth, SDKs, and an official MCP server make integration straightforward for both direct and partner use cases.
blocker: OAuth app integration requires Klaviyo tech partner enrollment for production use; free plan caps email sends and limits post-60-day support to community/self-service.
ev: Klaviyo developer docs confirm private key and OAuth auth flows; klaviyo.com/newsroom/mcp-server and developers.klaviyo.com/en/docs/klaviyo_mcp_server confirm official vendor MCP GA August 2025; pricing page confirms free self-serve tier.

name: systeme.io
what: All-in-one online marketing platform (sales funnels, email marketing, membership sites, courses, affiliates) with a public REST API and an official MCP server for contact management.
auth: API key (generated in account Settings > Public API Keys; self-serve, no approval required)
access_note: Free-forever plan available; API keys created instantly in dashboard settings.
api: RESTful public API documented at https://developer.systeme.io; covers contacts, tags, funnels, orders, and more.
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists (documented at https://help.systeme.io/article/9489-how-to-use-systeme-ios-mcp); currently focused on contact management (contacts, tags, custom fields).
build: Obtain an API key from Settings > Public API Keys; use Bearer token auth on REST endpoints at developer.systeme.io. MCP server available for AI assistant integration via official help docs.
blocker: None significant; free plan users have full API access. MCP server scope is currently limited to contact management.
ev: Multiple third-party integration docs (Make.com, APIx-Drive, MindCloud) confirm self-serve API key flow. Official help article and YouTube video from systeme.io confirm vendor-run MCP server.

name: Pinterest
what: Pinterest visual discovery and social media platform with a full REST API (v5) for pins, boards, ads, catalogs, analytics, and trends
auth: OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow; scopes required per resource; access tokens generated via OAuth
access_note: Trial access is self-serve after developer app approval; Standard (production) access requires a separate upgrade review/approval process through Pinterest developer support
api: Yes — Pinterest API v5 at https://developers.pinterest.com; covers pins, boards, ads, catalogs, analytics, trends
mcp: Community/third-party only (e.g., collactivelabs/pinterest-mcp-server, terryso/mcp-pinterest on GitHub); Pinterest's internal MCP ecosystem is for their own engineering teams, not a public vendor-run MCP server for external developers
build: Yes — well-documented REST API with OAuth quickstart, official GitHub repo (pinterest/api-quickstart), and developer portal
blocker: Standard (full production) access requires manual approval/upgrade beyond trial; trial is limited to testing before product is ready for end users
ev: developers.pinterest.com docs, GitHub pinterest/api-quickstart, blotato.com API pricing guide, Pinterest Business Community upgrade thread, AdWeek API v5 launch article, InfoQ Pinterest internal MCP article

name: Threads (Meta)
what: Threads (Meta) social media API — allows developers to publish posts, manage replies, read user content, search, and access analytics on the Threads platform
auth: OAuth 2.0 with Threads user access tokens (short-lived or long-lived, app-scoped); Meta App Review required for production access
access_note: Requires Meta developer app creation plus Meta App Review and permission approval before production use; development/testing access is available without review but rate-limited
api: REST API (official Meta Threads API); endpoints for publishing, replies, insights, search, webhooks, polls, location tags, GIF support, and more; documented at developers.facebook.com/docs/threads
mcp: community only — no official Meta/Threads MCP server found; community MCP servers exist (e.g., baguskto/threads-mcp on GitHub indexed by LobeHub, and a Medium article describing a community MCP server for Threads content creation)
build: Feasible with official REST API + OAuth 2.0; Meta App Review is the main gate for production; SDKs and clients exist in multiple languages (community-built); rate limits tied to account impressions
blocker: Meta App Review gating for production access; rate limits based on account impression count; no official paid tier to raise limits
ev: developers.facebook.com/documentation/threads/get-started confirms OAuth 2.0 token auth; blotato.com confirms free but gated by App Review and rate-limited; lobehub.com and medium.com confirm community MCP servers only

name: SendGrid
what: Email delivery and marketing campaigns API platform (transactional email + marketing automation), owned by Twilio
auth: API key (self-generated in dashboard); OAuth also supported for some integrations
access_note: Free trial available with no credit card required; paid plans scale by email volume
api: REST API v3; full reference at https://docs.sendgrid.com/api-reference
mcp: Community/third-party only — two open-source MCP servers found on GitHub (garethcull/sendgrid-mcp, Garoth/sendgrid-mcp); no official Twilio/SendGrid-run MCP server found
build: Well-documented REST API with SDKs (Node, Python, Ruby, Java, Go, PHP, C#); Twilio docs at https://www.twilio.com/docs/sendgrid
blocker: None significant; API keys are instantly self-provisioned, no approval gate
ev: Multiple official Twilio/SendGrid docs snippets confirming API key auth and self-serve signup; community MCP servers confirmed on GitHub; no official vendor MCP found

name: Shopify
what: Shopify is a leading ecommerce platform offering REST and GraphQL Admin APIs, Storefront API, and more for building apps and integrations with online stores.
auth: OAuth 2.0 (Authorization Code Grant) for public/custom apps; API key + secret for private apps. Scopes required per resource.
access_note: Developers can register at partners.shopify.com and obtain API credentials immediately; some app listing features require Shopify Partner approval.
api: Yes — REST Admin API, GraphQL Admin API, Storefront API, and others documented at https://shopify.dev/docs/api
mcp: Community/unofficial only — multiple community MCP servers exist (e.g., antoineschaller/shopify-mcp-server, ajackus-shopify-mcp-server on npm); no official Shopify-run MCP server confirmed in evidence.
build: Extensive official SDKs (Ruby, Node, Python), Partner Dashboard, webhooks, CLI tooling, and billing APIs available.
blocker: None significant — free self-serve developer access; production app distribution via Shopify App Store may require review.
ev: Strong evidence from shopify.dev official docs, GitHub Shopify org OAuth docs, and multiple third-party references confirming OAuth2 flow and self-serve access.

name: WooCommerce
what: WooCommerce is an open-source ecommerce plugin for WordPress, providing a REST API for managing products, orders, customers, coupons, and more via CRUD operations.
auth: Consumer Key / Consumer Secret (API keys generated in WooCommerce admin); supports OAuth 1.0a or Basic Auth over HTTPS
access_note: API keys are generated directly in the WooCommerce admin dashboard by any store owner; no approval process required.
api: REST API (wc/v3); full CRUD on products, orders, customers, categories, coupons, etc. Documented at https://developer.woocommerce.com/docs/apis/rest-api/
mcp: Official WooCommerce MCP server exists (beta); documented at https://developer.woocommerce.com/docs/features/mcp and builds on WordPress Core MCP adapter. Community/third-party MCP servers also exist (techspawn/woocommerce-mcp-server on GitHub, StackOne).
build: Straightforward to integrate: generate API keys in WooCommerce admin, use consumer key/secret with any HTTP client or official SDKs (PHP, Python, Ruby, Node). Well-documented REST API with broad language support.
blocker: Requires access to a WooCommerce store (self-hosted WordPress); no SaaS central endpoint — each store has its own URL. HTTPS required for Basic Auth; OAuth needed for HTTP.
ev: Official WooCommerce developer docs confirm REST API authentication, key generation, and MCP integration. Multiple corroborating third-party sources.

name: BigCommerce
what: BigCommerce is a leading SaaS ecommerce platform offering REST and GraphQL APIs to manage products, orders, customers, and store settings programmatically.
auth: OAuth 2.0 (store-level, app-level, and account-level API accounts); access token passed via X-Auth-Token header for direct API calls
access_note: Developers can create a free sandbox store and obtain API credentials directly from the BigCommerce developer portal without sales approval.
api: REST APIs and GraphQL APIs; well-documented at developer.bigcommerce.com
mcp: Community/third-party MCP servers exist (isaacgounton/bigcommerce-api-mcp on GitHub, StackOne's BigCommerce MCP, mcpmarket.com listing) but no official vendor-run MCP server found in evidence.
build: Yes — full REST and GraphQL API suite, OAuth-based authentication, sandbox store available, extensive developer docs at docs.bigcommerce.com
blocker: None identified; API is openly accessible to registered developers with sandbox support.
ev: Multiple BigCommerce official docs pages (docs.bigcommerce.com/developer) confirm OAuth API accounts and self-serve sandbox creation; community MCP servers confirmed via GitHub and third-party listings.

name: Salesforce Commerce Cloud
what: Salesforce Commerce Cloud (Agentforce Commerce) is a cloud-based ecommerce platform enabling B2C/B2B online storefronts, merchandising, and order management, with APIs (SCAPI, OCAPI) for developer integration.
auth: OAuth 2.0 (client credentials and PKCE flows via Salesforce Account Manager / Connected Apps; Consumer Key + Secret for OCAPI; SLAS for SCAPI)
access_note: Full Commerce Cloud requires a paid Salesforce license/contract; free trials are offered for exploration but production access is gated. Developer sandbox access typically requires an existing org.
api: Yes — B2C Commerce API (SCAPI) and Open Commerce API (OCAPI) documented at https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/commerce
mcp: Community/third-party only — BrinsilElias/commercecloud-mcp-server and taurgis/sfcc-dev-mcp exist on GitHub; Salesforce's own hosted MCP servers are noted as 'currently in pilot' (not GA for Commerce Cloud specifically). No official vendor-run Commerce Cloud MCP server confirmed.
build: Builders can integrate via SCAPI/OCAPI REST APIs using OAuth 2.0; SDKs and PWA Kit available; MCP community servers available for AI tooling; prebuilt connectors exist in iPaaS platforms (Patchworks, Nexla).
blocker: Production API access requires a paid Commerce Cloud license; sandbox/developer environments tied to org provisioning; OCAPI requires certificate-based setup for some flows.
ev: Salesforce developer docs confirm OAuth 2.0 auth flows; mcpservers.org and GitHub confirm community MCP servers; Cirra article notes Salesforce hosted MCP is pilot-only; free trial availability confirmed on salesforce.com.

name: Magento (Adobe Commerce)
what: Adobe Commerce (Magento) is an enterprise ecommerce platform offering REST and GraphQL APIs for storefront, catalog, orders, customers, and payments management, available as open source (Magento Open Source) and cloud-hosted (Adobe Commerce on Cloud).
auth: OAuth 2.0 via Adobe IMS for Adobe Commerce as a Cloud Service; OAuth 1.0a token exchange or admin/customer token-based auth for on-premises/self-hosted deployments
access_note: Magento Open Source is freely downloadable; Adobe Commerce cloud plans require contacting Adobe sales, but the REST/GraphQL APIs are accessible to all deployments without a separate approval gate
api: Comprehensive REST API (versioned, e.g. /rest/V1/) and GraphQL API; documented at https://developer.adobe.com/commerce/webapi/
mcp: Community/third-party MCP servers exist (e.g. rafaelstz/adobe-commerce-dev-mcp on GitHub, LobeHub Magento MCP, StackOne Magento MCP with 128 actions, mcpmarket.com listing); no official Adobe/Magento vendor-run MCP server confirmed
build: Use REST or GraphQL APIs with OAuth 2.0 (IMS) or token auth; extensive SDK/extension ecosystem via Commerce Marketplace; Composio toolkit not available
blocker: Adobe Commerce cloud (PaaS) licensing requires enterprise sales engagement; on-premises/Open Source has no gating. IMS OAuth setup required for cloud service API calls.
ev: Official API docs at developer.adobe.com/commerce/webapi confirm REST and GraphQL availability; auth page confirms OAuth 2.0 via IMS for cloud service; multiple community MCP servers found but no vendor-official MCP

name: Squarespace
what: Website builder and ecommerce platform with Commerce APIs for managing orders, products, inventory, transactions, contacts, and webhooks
auth: API key or OAuth 2.0 (registered OAuth partner for third-party apps; API key for personal use)
access_note: API keys are self-serve via Squarespace account settings; OAuth requires registering as a developer partner via developers.squarespace.com
api: REST API documented at developers.squarespace.com; supports Commerce APIs (Orders, Inventory, Transactions, Contacts), Webhooks, and site information endpoints
mcp: Community/third-party only (GitHub: BusyBee3333/squarespace-mcp-2026-complete and mcpbundles.com listing); no official vendor-run MCP server found. Note: Square MCP is a separate company (squareup.com).
build: Viable — well-documented REST API with OAuth and API key auth, webhooks, and multiple ecommerce endpoints; good ecosystem of integration examples
blocker: OAuth partner registration may require approval for third-party app publishing; API key access is limited to a single website per key
ev: developers.squarespace.com (official docs), support.squarespace.com/hc/en-us/articles/236297987 (API keys), GitHub community MCP repo

name: Ecwid
what: Ecwid (by Lightspeed) is a hosted ecommerce platform allowing merchants to create and manage online stores; its REST API enables programmatic access to store data, orders, products, payments, and storefront settings.
auth: OAuth 2.0 (authorization code flow for multi-store apps; simplified single-store token for custom integrations); Bearer token used in API calls
access_note: API access requires a paid Ecwid plan (Venture, Business, or Unlimited); Free plan merchants cannot use the API. Developer account signup is self-serve, but app publishing requires Ecwid team review.
api: REST API documented at https://docs.ecwid.com/api-reference and https://api-docs.ecwid.com; supports CRUD on products, orders, customers, payments, shipping, and storefront settings.
mcp: Community/third-party only — viasocket.com lists an MCP server for Ecwid, but no official Ecwid/Lightspeed-run MCP server is evidenced.
build: Developers sign up, create an app client, implement OAuth 2.0, and call the REST API. Apps intended for the Ecwid App Market require submission and review by the Ecwid Apps team.
blocker: Free-tier merchants are blocked from API access; paid plan required on the merchant side. App marketplace listing requires vendor approval.
ev: Ecwid official docs (docs.ecwid.com/api-reference, docs.ecwid.com/launch-apps/native-and-external-apps), GitHub ecwid-api-docs repo (overview + authentication files) confirm OAuth 2.0, paid-plan gating, and developer program details.

name: Gumroad
what: Gumroad is an ecommerce platform for creators to sell digital products, memberships, and software directly to audiences.
auth: OAuth2 and API key (access token) via Gumroad account settings
access_note: API keys and OAuth2 credentials are generated directly from a Gumroad account with no approval gate required.
api: REST API v2 documented at https://app.gumroad.com/api; supports products, sales, subscribers, license verification, offers, and more.
mcp: Community/third-party MCP servers exist (e.g., github.com/rmarescu/gumroad-mcp, Composio toolkit page) but no official vendor-run MCP server found in evidence.
build: Straightforward to integrate: self-serve OAuth2/API key auth, well-documented REST API, supported by n8n, Make, NoCodeAPI, and community Node.js clients.
blocker: No significant blockers; API rate limits noted in third-party docs but official limits not detailed in evidence.
ev: Evidence from n8n docs, Make/apps.make.com, nocodeapi, GitHub community clients, and mcp.so community MCP listings. Official API docs URL confirmed in hint.

name: Amazon Selling Partner
what: Amazon Selling Partner API (SP-API) is a REST-based API that allows Amazon selling partners and third-party developers to programmatically access data on orders, shipments, payments, inventory, and more within Amazon's marketplace ecosystem.
auth: OAuth 2.0 via Login with Amazon (LWA); requires LWA client credentials plus AWS IAM role/STS for request signing
access_note: Requires a Professional Seller Central account, registration of a developer profile, and Amazon approval of the application before production API access is granted; sandbox available after registration
api: REST-based; full SP-API documented at https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api
mcp: Community/unofficial only; multiple community MCP servers exist (e.g., github.com/jay-trivedi/amazon_sp_mcp, github.com/mattcoatsworth/AmazonSeller-mcp-server) but no official Amazon-run MCP server found in evidence
build: Well-documented REST API with OAuth 2.0 + AWS SigV4 signing; SDKs available (e.g., python-amazon-sp-api); prescriptive guidance published on AWS docs; integration complexity is moderate-to-high due to auth requirements
blocker: Gated access: must have a Professional Seller Central account, create a developer profile, and receive Amazon approval; OAuth + AWS IAM dual-auth adds implementation complexity
ev: HIGH — official Amazon developer docs at developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api and sell.amazon.com/developers confirm requirements; AWS prescriptive guidance confirms OAuth 2.0 + LWA model

name: fanbasis
what: FanBasis is a payment orchestration and subscription management platform for ecommerce/creator businesses, offering multi-processor failover, BNPL suite, dispute resolution, and subscription recovery.
auth: API key via x-api-key header

name: Sherlock

mcp: Community MCP server exists (github.com/Burnsedia/sherlock-mcp and Sherlock Domains MCP on explainx.ai/LangDB), but NO official vendor-run MCP server. Vendor is an open-source project with no commercial entity.
build: Integrate by: (1) running Sherlock CLI locally/in Docker and parsing stdout, (2) using Apify actor REST API with Apify token, or (3) deploying the community sherlock-mcp FastMCP server for LLM tool use.
blocker: No official API or SDK; must rely on CLI subprocess calls, third-party Apify wrapper, or community MCP. No rate limits on the OSS tool itself, but target sites may block scraping.
ev: GitHub repo at github.com/sherlock-project/sherlock confirms open-source CLI. Apify actor at apify.com/misceres/sherlock confirms third-party API wrapper. Community MCP at github.com/Burnsedia/sherlock-mcp confirmed by search snippet.
