mcp: Official vendor MCP server documented at developers.asana.com/docs/mcp-server and listed on AWS Marketplace; accessible via app integration
build: Sign up free, generate PAT in developer console, or register OAuth app; MCP server available via app integration
blocker: None; fully self-serve API and MCP access
ev: developers.asana.com/docs/personal-access-token, developers.asana.com/docs/authentication, developers.asana.com/docs/mcp-server, aws.amazon.com/marketplace/pp/prodview-6f7zmapqyfwho

name: Monday.com
what: Work operating system / project management platform with boards, workflows, automations, and CRM; exposes a GraphQL API and official MCP server for AI agents
auth: API token (per-user, generated in account settings) or OAuth 2.0 (register app in Monday developer portal); guests must use OAuth or shortLivedToken
access_note: Free plan available, no credit card required; API access included on Free; 14-day Pro trial also offered
api: GraphQL API (versioned); REST-style endpoints also available; full docs at https://developer.monday.com/api-reference
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists; monday.com runs a hosted MCP server enabling AI agents (Claude, ChatGPT, Copilot, Cursor, custom) to access workspace data; documented at https://developer.monday.com/api-reference/docs/monday-mcp-security-overview and https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/32364501317906-monday-MCP-available-tools
build: Build directly against the GraphQL API using an API token or OAuth 2.0; connect AI agents via the official MCP server; SDK/app framework available via Monday developer portal
blocker: None significant; free tier with full API access available without credit card
ev: Multiple official Monday developer docs confirm self-serve API token generation, OAuth 2.0 support, free plan with API access, and an official vendor-run MCP server

name: ClickUp
what: ClickUp is a cloud-based project management and productivity platform offering tasks, docs, goals, whiteboards, and automation.
auth: Personal API Token or OAuth 2.0
access_note: Free plan available; API accessible to all users. SCIM requires Enterprise plan.
api: REST API at https://developer.clickup.com; supports tasks, custom fields, views, docs, webhooks, and more.
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists — documented at https://developer.clickup.com/docs/connect-an-ai-assistant-to-clickups-mcp-server
build: Full REST API with OAuth 2.0 and personal tokens; webhooks supported; well-documented with developer portal and community forum.
blocker: None significant; SCIM provisioning gated to Enterprise tier.
ev: ClickUp developer docs confirm OAuth 2.0 and personal token auth; official MCP server page confirmed at developer.clickup.com; self-serve free plan confirmed via multiple sources.

name: Coda
what: Coda is a collaborative doc/database platform (now rebranded as 'Superhuman Docs') combining documents, tables, and automations for team productivity and project management.
auth: Bearer token (personal API token generated in Coda Account Settings > API settings)
access_note: API tokens can be generated directly by any Coda user from their account settings with no approval required.
api: REST API v1 documented at https://coda.io/developers/apis/v1; supports docs, pages, tables, rows, columns, and more.
mcp: Community MCP servers exist (e.g., github.com/orellazri/coda-mcp, adeel0x01-coda-mcp-server on LobeHub); no official vendor-run MCP server found in evidence.
build: Use the REST API with Bearer token auth; well-documented endpoints for reading/writing docs, tables, and rows. Python and other ecosystem integrations available (e.g., dlt hub). No official SDK found in evidence but third-party wrappers exist.
blocker: Rate limits apply (not quantified in evidence); Enterprise Packs and certain features require paid/enterprise plans.
ev: Vendor API docs hint confirmed (coda.io/developers/apis/v1); token generation flow confirmed via Stitchflow guide; MCP servers confirmed as community-built via GitHub and mcpservers.org listings.

name: Smartsheet
what: Smartsheet is a cloud-based work management and collaboration platform offering sheets, reports, dashboards, and automation for project/task management.
auth: API key (access token generated in Smartsheet UI) or OAuth 2.0 for third-party app integrations
access_note: Free trial available; paid plans required after trial. API access is available to developers self-serve via the developer portal at developers.smartsheet.com.
api: REST API documented at https://smartsheet.redoc.ly and https://developers.smartsheet.com; supports sheets, rows, columns, workspaces, reports, attachments, discussions, automations, and users.
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists; Smartsheet published its own MCP Server (open, protocol-based) documented at developers.smartsheet.com/ai-mcp/smartsheet/mcp-server-tools and help.smartsheet.com/articles/2483670-smartsheet-mcp-server.
build: Well-documented REST API with OpenAPI spec, OAuth 2.0 support, webhooks, and an official MCP server. SDKs and guides available. Strong foundation for integration.
blocker: API rate limits and plan-tier capacity constraints apply; Smartsheet Gov and Regions Europe use separate tokens. No significant blockers for standard commercial use.
ev: Multiple official Smartsheet developer docs pages confirm API key + OAuth 2.0 auth. Official MCP server confirmed by smartsheet.com content-center announcement, help.smartsheet.com article, and developers.smartsheet.com MCP tools page.

name: Harvest
what: Time tracking and project management SaaS with a REST API (v2) for managing time entries, projects, clients, invoices, and reports
auth: OAuth 2.0 (Authorization Code flow recommended for server-side apps) or Personal Access Tokens for simple integrations
access_note: Developers can register an OAuth app or generate a Personal Access Token directly from their Harvest account settings; no approval gate required
api: REST API v2 (https://help.getharvest.com/api-v2/); well-documented with full endpoint coverage
mcp: Community MCP servers exist (taiste/harvest-mcp-server on GitHub, ianaleck/harvest-mcp-server, valtirallc-harvest-mcp-server on LobeHub) but no official vendor-run MCP server found
build: Straightforward: obtain a Personal Access Token or register an OAuth app, then call REST endpoints; multiple community MCP servers available for AI assistant integration
blocker: None significant; API access requires a Harvest account (free tier available); some endpoints may depend on plan level (Pro/Premium)
ev: Harvest's own docs confirm OAuth 2.0 and PAT auth; self-serve app registration described at help.getharvest.com/api-v2/; multiple community MCP repos found but no official vendor MCP

name: Stripe
what: Stripe is a payment processing and financial infrastructure platform offering APIs for payments, billing, subscriptions, Connect (marketplace/platform payments), and more.
auth: API Key (Bearer token, sk_test_* or sk_live_*) and OAuth 2.0 (via Stripe Apps/Connect). API key sent as Authorization: Bearer {api_key}.
access_note: Developers can sign up and obtain API keys immediately at stripe.com; no sales gating for standard API access.
api: Comprehensive REST API documented at https://docs.stripe.com/api; official SDKs for multiple languages; supports API keys and OAuth 2.0.
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists: Stripe hosts a remote MCP server (stripe-mcp) confirmed by lobehub.com and glama.ai snippets referencing 'Stripe hosts a remote MCP server'. Vendor-run.
build: Use Stripe API keys or OAuth 2.0 (Stripe Apps). REST API with official libraries (Node, Python, Ruby, PHP, Go, Java, .NET). Webhooks for event-driven flows. Well-documented at docs.stripe.com.
blocker: None for standard use. Live mode keys require account activation and business verification. Stripe Connect/marketplace features may require additional review.
ev: Strong evidence: official Stripe docs (docs.stripe.com/api, docs.stripe.com/stripe-apps/api-authentication), Nexla docs confirming Bearer auth, Composio auth page confirming both API Key and OAuth2, lobehub/glama confirming official Stripe MCP server.

name: Plaid
what: Plaid is a financial data network API that enables applications to connect users' bank accounts, access transaction data, verify identity, and perform financial operations. It serves as middleware between apps and financial institutions.
auth: API key authentication using client_id + secret passed in headers or request body; OAuth 2.0 used for end-user bank authorization flows (not for developer API auth itself)
access_note: Free Trial plan available (no business registration, contract, or security questionnaire required) for up to 10 Production Items. Pay-as-you-go and subscription plans also available; US/Canada accounts created on or after April 15, 2026 get Trial plan automatically.
api: REST API with JSON payloads; endpoints use PLAID-CLIENT-ID and PLAID-SECRET headers; Sandbox environment available for free testing before production
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists. Plaid offers two MCP servers: (1) Dashboard MCP server — a remote MCP server hosted by Plaid for Production diagnostics, debugging Items, Link conversion analytics, and usage metrics; (2) a second MCP server (likely local/API-facing). Announced via Plaid blog and documented at plaid.com/docs/resources/mcp. Initially integrated with Anthropic's Claude.
build: Sign up for free Trial plan at plaid.com, obtain client_id and secret from the Dashboard, use Sandbox for development, integrate REST API or official SDKs (Node, Python, Java, etc.), implement Plaid Link for end-user bank connection UI
blocker: Trial plan limited to 10 Production Items; scaling requires paid plan. Some products or institutions may require upgraded plans. Non-US/Canada regions may have different access rules.
ev: Plaid docs confirm self-serve Trial plan, API key auth model, pricing tiers, and two official MCP servers with Plaid blog post and dedicated docs page (plaid.com/docs/resources/mcp).

name: Binance
what: Binance cryptocurrency exchange platform offering REST and WebSocket APIs for spot trading, futures, account management, market data, and OAuth2 login integration for third-party apps.
auth: API key passed via X-MBX-APIKEY header for REST calls; HMAC/RSA/Ed25519 signature for signed endpoints; OAuth2 available for user-delegated access via Binance Open Platform.
access_note: API keys generated self-serve in the Binance account dashboard; no approval gate documented in evidence.
api: REST + WebSocket APIs; official docs at https://binance-docs.github.io/apidocs/ and https://developers.binance.com/en; Binance.US variant at https://docs.binance.us.
mcp: Community/third-party only. Evidence shows TermiX-official/binance-mcp on GitHub and binance-futures-mcp on PyPI — no official Binance-run MCP server found.
build: Yes — well-documented public API, official GitHub org (github.com/binance), multiple SDKs (python-binance etc.), OAuth2 for third-party login, developer center at developers.binance.com.
blocker: Geographic restrictions (e.g., Binance.com unavailable to US users; separate Binance.US API). KYC may be required for certain trading endpoints. Rate limits apply.
ev: Multiple official doc URLs confirmed (binance-docs.github.io, developers.binance.com, docs.binance.us, GitHub binance org); auth mechanism clearly documented in snippets.

name: Paygent Connect
what: Paygent Connect is a Japanese payment gateway service operated by Paygent Co., Ltd. (paygent.co.jp), supporting card payments, convenience store payments, and other methods for Japanese merchants. It is listed as a supported gateway in Google Pay API.
auth: API key-based authentication is typical for Japanese payment gateways; Paygent is referenced in Google Pay API docs as a gateway partner. Exact auth mechanism for Paygent Connect API not confirmed from evidence.
access_note: Japanese payment gateway services typically require merchant registration, contract, and approval before API access is granted. No self-serve sandbox evidence found.
api: REST API likely exists (referenced as a Google Pay gateway with gatewayMerchantId parameter), but direct API documentation URL for paygent.co.jp was not surfaced in evidence.
mcp: none known (unverified)
build: Possible to build integrations via their payment API and Google Pay gateway integration; no SDK or developer portal confirmed from evidence.
blocker: Limited English documentation; Japanese-market-focused service likely requires a Japanese merchant account and contract. API docs not directly surfaced in search evidence.
ev: Google Pay API docs confirm 'paygent' as a supported gateway token. A separate 'paygent.to' / 'withpaygent.com' entity appears to be a different AI billing product unrelated to paygent.co.jp.

name: iPayX
what: iPayX Protocol is a FINTRAC-registered FX forensic audit layer that detects hidden bank FX spreads by comparing applied rates against real-time mid-market rates, serving fintech sales teams and FX brokers.
auth: Unknown/unverified; no direct API auth documentation found in evidence. General OAuth/API key patterns exist in industry but iPayX-specific auth method not documented in retrieved snippets.
access_note: Pricing page exists (ipayx.ai/pricing) suggesting commercial access; no evidence of a public self-serve free tier or sandbox. FINTRAC-registered MSB implies regulated onboarding.
api: Likely REST API exists given integrations page (ipayx.ai/integrations) and MCP server presence, but no public API docs URL confirmed in evidence.
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists: 'Ipayx Fx Audit MCP Server' created by iPAYX-Technologies on chat.mcp.so (~2 months ago), detecting hidden FX spreads with 0-10 scoring.
build: Yes — integrations page and official MCP server indicate third-party/AI integration is supported.
blocker: No public API documentation found; regulated fintech onboarding likely required; auth mechanism unconfirmed.
ev: ipayx.ai/pricing, ipayx.ai/integrations, chat.mcp.so/server/ipayx-fx-audit/iPAYX-Technologies

name: QuickBooks
what: QuickBooks Online is Intuit's cloud-based accounting/bookkeeping software for small businesses, offering invoicing, expense tracking, payroll, and financial reporting via a REST API.
auth: OAuth 2.0 via Intuit's identity platform; requires Client ID and Client Secret obtained from the Intuit Developer Portal; supports sandbox and production environments.
access_note: Developers can self-register at developer.intuit.com, create an app, and obtain credentials immediately; sandbox access is free.
api: REST API (QuickBooks Online Accounting API) at developer.intuit.com; well-documented with SDKs, OAuth 2.0 Playground, and sandbox environment.
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists: github.com/intuit/quickbooks-online-mcp-server — published by Intuit under MIT license.
build: Straightforward to integrate; OAuth 2.0 flow is standard but noted by community as moderately complex to implement; comprehensive docs and SDKs available; sandbox available for testing.
blocker: Apps accessing live customer data require Intuit's app review/approval process before going to production; OAuth token refresh management adds implementation overhead.
ev: Multiple authoritative sources confirm OAuth 2.0 auth (developer.intuit.com official docs, getknit.dev, apideck.com); official MCP server confirmed via github.com/intuit/quickbooks-online-mcp-server; self-serve developer portal confirmed.

name: Xero
what: Xero is a cloud-based accounting and financial management platform for small and medium businesses, offering invoicing, payroll, bank reconciliation, and reporting via a REST API.
auth: OAuth 2.0 (authorization code flow and PKCE flow); client_id and client_secret obtained by registering an app at developer.xero.com; access tokens expire in 30 minutes with refresh token support
access_note: Free developer account via self sign-up at developer.xero.com; tiered pricing plans available for production/app store listing
api: REST API with OpenAPI spec (XeroAPI/Xero-OpenAPI on GitHub); official SDKs for Node, Python, Java, .NET, PHP, Ruby; API Explorer available in browser
mcp: Official vendor MCP server: github.com/xeroapi/xero-mcp-server — launched March 26, 2025, maintained by XeroAPI organization
build: Register app at developer.xero.com to get client_id/secret; implement OAuth 2.0 code or PKCE flow; use official SDKs or OpenAPI-generated clients; MCP server available for AI agent integrations
blocker: None significant; OAuth 2.0 required (no basic API key auth); production apps targeting Xero App Store listing require additional review/listing process
ev: developer.xero.com official docs confirm OAuth 2.0 self-serve registration; GitHub xeroapi org hosts official SDKs and MCP server; Grokipedia and mcp.so confirm official MCP server by Xero launched 2025-03-26

name: Brex
what: Brex is a corporate card and spend management platform for businesses, offering APIs for managing cards, expenses, budgets, spend limits, payments, and team financial data.
auth: OAuth 2.0 (recommended, with Dynamic Client Registration per RFC 7591) or static API token (machine-to-machine); token created in Brex Dashboard > Developer > Settings
access_note: API is free to all Brex customers; developer agreement must be accepted in dashboard before use; token creation is self-serve via dashboard
api: Yes — REST API documented at https://developer.brex.com; covers accounts, cards, expenses, budgets, spend limits, payments, team management
mcp: Official vendor MCP server at https://developer.brex.com/docs/mcp; supports OAuth and Dynamic Client Registration; also referenced on brex.com/support/using-brex-in-ai-apps
build: Well-documented REST API with OAuth 2.0 and static token auth; official MCP server available; SDKs noted; Zapier integration exists; third-party connectors (Truto, Stitchflow) available
blocker: Must be an existing Brex customer to obtain API access; must accept developer API agreement in dashboard
ev: developer.brex.com/docs/mcp confirms official MCP server; brex.com/support/brex-api and businesswire.com announcement confirm self-serve free API for all customers; stitchflow.com documents OAuth 2.0 and static token setup

name: Ramp
what: Ramp is a corporate finance platform (corporate cards, expense management, bill pay, procurement) offering a REST Developer API for customers and partners to access transactions, cards, users, and more.
auth: OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow (client_id + client_secret → /oauth/token); scopes configured per app
access_note: Production API access requires a Ramp account; sandbox access is explicitly not self-serve and requires contacting an account manager for provisioning.
api: REST API documented at https://docs.ramp.com/developer-api/v1; supports transactions, cards, users, departments, bills, reimbursements, and partner/application flows.
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists: github.com/ramp-public/ramp_mcp, maintained by Ramp Business Corporation; implements ETL pipeline with ephemeral SQLite for LLM integration.
build: Create credentials in the Ramp dashboard (or via partner program), configure OAuth app with desired scopes, obtain access token via client credentials flow, call REST endpoints.
blocker: Sandbox environment is gated — must contact Ramp account manager for access; production API requires an active Ramp business account.
ev: docs.ramp.com quickstart and sandbox access support article confirm OAuth flow and gated sandbox; github.com/ramp-public/ramp_mcp confirms official vendor MCP.

name: PitchBook
what: PitchBook is a private capital market data platform providing intelligence on companies, deals, investors, and funds for finance professionals, VCs, and investors.
auth: API access uses OAuth and/or API key authentication based on third-party integration evidence; official auth method not fully documented publicly.
access_note: API/data feeds are enterprise products requiring a paid subscription and sales engagement; no self-serve developer sandbox found. Access is bundled with PitchBook platform subscriptions.
api: REST API exists (endpoints documented on dlthub/apitracker including companies, deals, investors); not publicly open — requires a PitchBook data license.
mcp: Official vendor MCP server: PitchBook announced and launched an official MCP/ChatGPT integration (PitchBook app in ChatGPT via MCP) per November 2025 press release and LinkedIn posts.
build: Builders with a PitchBook data license can integrate via REST API (OAuth/key auth). Unified.to offers a third-party unified API wrapper. No public free tier or sandbox.
blocker: Access to the API requires an enterprise PitchBook subscription; no self-serve sign-up for API credentials. Pricing and access are gated behind sales.
ev: Evidence from dlthub REST endpoint docs, apitracker profile, PitchBook press release (Nov 2025) on MCP/ChatGPT integration, LinkedIn post confirming ChatGPT app launch, unified.to integration page.

name: NotebookLM
what: NotebookLM is Google's AI-powered research and note-taking assistant that can synthesize sources, generate summaries, and produce audio overviews (podcasts). An Enterprise/API tier exists under Google Cloud.
auth: OAuth2 / Bearer token via Google Cloud (gcloud auth); Enterprise API uses service account or access token. Consumer product uses Google account SSO.
access_note: NotebookLM Enterprise API (notebooks.create etc.) requires Google Cloud enrollment and NotebookLM Enterprise access—not freely self-serve. Consumer NotebookLM has no official public API; community/unofficial REST wrappers exist (gnh1201, roomi-fields) that scrape or automate the UI.
api: Official REST API exists for NotebookLM Enterprise on Google Cloud (docs.cloud.google.com/gemini/enterprise/notebooklm-enterprise). No official public API for the consumer product. Unofficial community APIs and automation tools exist.
mcp: Community/unofficial only. Multiple community MCP servers exist (kkaminsk/NotebookLM-MCP, moodrobotics/notebooklm-mcp-server, PleasePrompot on mcp.directory, mcpmarket.com) but none are official Google/NotebookLM vendor-run MCP servers.
build: Can build against NotebookLM Enterprise API with Google Cloud credentials. Consumer product has no sanctioned API; unofficial tools use browser automation. Enterprise API supports notebook CRUD and audio generation.
blocker: Enterprise API requires Google Cloud NotebookLM Enterprise subscription (gated/paid). Consumer NotebookLM API access is unsanctioned—unofficial wrappers risk breakage and ToS issues.
ev: Google Cloud docs confirm Enterprise REST API with Bearer token auth. Multiple community MCP repos confirm no official vendor MCP. Consumer product pages show no developer API offering.

name: Otter AI
what: Otter.ai is an AI-powered meeting transcription, note-taking, and conversation intelligence platform with a public API and MCP server.
auth: API key (generated from account dashboard under Integrations > Developer tab)
access_note: API key can be created directly from any Otter.ai account without gating; free tier exists, paid plans from $19.99/month/user.
api: REST API with webhooks; public API documented at help.otter.ai; supports action items, transcripts, and related meeting data.
mcp: Official vendor MCP server (Otter MCP Server documented at help.otter.ai/hc/en-us/articles/35287607569687); Otter functions as both MCP client and server.
build: Yes — public REST API with API key auth, webhooks, and an official MCP server for AI-to-AI integration.
blocker: None significant; API key is self-serve. Enterprise features (custom integrations/workflows) may require higher-tier plan.
ev: Otter.ai Help Center articles for Public API and MCP Server, otter.ai pricing page, nojitter.com coverage of MCP features.

name: Fathom
what: Fathom is an AI meeting recorder and analytics platform (fathom.video) with a public API for accessing meeting recordings, summaries, transcripts, and webhooks. Note: fathom.video (AI meeting notes), usefathom.com (privacy-friendly web analytics), and fathomthat.ai (text analysis) are three distinct products sharing the Fathom name.
auth: API key (Bearer token) for REST API; OAuth2 also supported per MindCloud docs
access_note: Free 14-day trial available with no billing info required; API access included with Fathom accounts; API limit plans available via changelog
api: Yes — public REST API documented at developers.fathom.ai with guides, API docs, SDK docs, and MCP section
mcp: Official MCP server referenced at developers.fathom.ai/mcp (vendor-hosted docs page listing MCP); community/third-party MCP servers also exist (Dot-Fun/fathom-mcp on GitHub for fathom.video, mackenly/mcp-fathom-analytics for usefathom.com)
build: REST API with SDKs; OAuth2 and API key auth; webhooks supported; llms.txt available for AI discovery
blocker: None identified; self-serve access with free trial
ev: developers.fathom.ai shows official API docs with MCP tab; GitHub repos confirm community MCP servers; usefathom.com changelog confirms API plans

name: Consensus
what: Consensus is an AI-powered academic research search engine (consensus.app) that surfaces evidence from peer-reviewed papers; distinct from goconsensus.com (demo/sales enablement tool). Both have APIs.
auth: API key (consensus.app API); OAuth 2.0 used for goconsensus.com CRM integrations
access_note: consensus.app API is 'available by application only' with custom pricing starting at $0.10/call plus platform fee; not self-serve sign-up
api: Yes — consensus.app offers a REST API (20 papers per query, $0.10/call baseline); goconsensus.com also has an API with documented credentials
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists — consensus.app runs an official remote MCP server documented at docs.consensus.app/docs/mcp and mcpservers.org/remote-mcp-servers/consensus, allowing AI assistants to query peer-reviewed research directly
build: Apply for API access at consensus.app/home/api; MCP endpoint available for AI assistant integration; no Composio toolkit found
blocker: API access is gated by application and custom pricing negotiation; not immediately self-serve
ev: consensus.app/home/api confirms application-only gated access and pricing; docs.consensus.app/docs/mcp and support.goconsensus.com/mcp-server-security-information confirm official MCP server; mcpservers.org confirms remote hosted MCP endpoint

name: Reducto
what: Agentic document platform providing REST API for parsing, extraction, classification, splitting, and workflow orchestration across PDFs, images, spreadsheets, DOCX, and 30+ formats
auth: API key (Bearer token); keys created in Studio dashboard, scoped per environment
access_note: Standard tier is pay-as-you-go with immediate API access; Growth/Enterprise tiers require contacting sales
api: REST API; full reference at docs.reducto.ai; endpoints include /parse, /extract, /split, /classify, and workflow orchestration
mcp: Official vendor MCP server at github.com/reductoai/mcp-server-reducto, documented at docs.reducto.ai/mcp-server; supports Claude Desktop, Cursor, VS Code Copilot, Windsurf, OpenAI Agents SDK
build: Integrate via REST API with API key auth; use official MCP server for agent/LLM tool-calling workflows; quickstart at docs.reducto.ai/quickstart
blocker: None for Standard tier; Growth/Enterprise require sales contact for trial
ev: docs.reducto.ai/agent-guide, docs.reducto.ai/quickstart, reducto.ai/pricing, github.com/reductoai/mcp-server-reducto, docs.reducto.ai/mcp-server

name: Devin
what: AI software engineer agent (Cognition's Devin) that autonomously performs coding, debugging, and engineering tasks via browser, terminal, and IDE.
auth: Bearer token via Service User API Key or Personal Access Token (PAT); Enterprise Admin uses Personal API Key (apk_user_*)
access_note: Free tier available; paid self-serve plans purchasable without talking to sales; Enterprise tier also available.
api: REST API documented at docs.devin.ai/api-reference; supports sessions, organizations, API key provisioning, and more.
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists (docs.devin.ai/work-with-devin/devin-mcp); also listed on Cognition's MCP Marketplace. Composio community wrapper also available.

blocker: Some endpoints (e.g., org member management) restricted to Enterprise Admins; PAT feature noted as Closed Beta.
ev: docs.devin.ai (official docs), cognition.com/blog/new-self-serve-plans-for-devin, cognition.com/blog/mcp-marketplace, xpay.sh pricing page

name: higgsfield
what: AI-powered video and image generation platform; API lets developers transform images/text into cinematic-grade videos, images, and speech
auth: API key via Bearer token in Authorization header; credentials (key or key+secret) obtained from Higgsfield Cloud dashboard
access_note: Free tier with 5–10 daily credits; paid plans available; no approval gate evident
api: REST API with official Python SDK (higgsfield-ai/higgsfield-client on GitHub); endpoints for text-to-video, image-to-video, text-to-image, speech synthesis
mcp: Community MCP servers exist (geopopos/geo_higgsfield_ai_mcp on GitHub, listed on LobeHub/explainx.ai); no official vendor-run MCP server confirmed
build: Use REST API with Bearer token auth; official Python SDK available; also accessible via MindCloud proxy or Make.com integration
blocker: None significant; free tier is credit-limited; no gating or waitlist detected
ev: Official Python SDK repo (github.com/higgsfield-ai/higgsfield-client), apidog blog detailing auth, unifuncs free tier details, community MCP repos on GitHub/LobeHub

name: Mermaid CLI
what: Mermaid CLI (mmdc) is an open-source command-line tool that converts Mermaid diagram definitions (text/markdown) into image files (SVG, PNG, PDF). It is a local CLI utility, not a hosted API service.
auth: No auth required; it is a local CLI tool installed via npm/PyPI and run on the user's machine. (Note: 'MERMAID API' snippets refer to a separate coral reef conservation project unrelated to mermaid-js.)
access_note: Freely available open-source tool on GitHub (mermaid-js/mermaid-cli) and npm/PyPI; no account or approval needed.
api: No REST/web API. It is invoked as a CLI command (mmdc). Can be used programmatically via Node.js child_process or wrapper libraries.
mcp: No official vendor MCP server. Multiple community MCP servers exist (peng-shawn/mermaid-mcp-server, rtuin/mcp-mermaid-validator, narasimhaponnada/mermaid-mcp, etc.) that wrap mermaid-cli to generate/validate diagrams.
build: Integrate by installing via npm ('npm install -g @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli') and invoking 'mmdc -i input.mmd -o output.svg'. Can also be called from Node.js scripts. Community MCP servers available for AI assistant integration.
blocker: Requires a local Node.js environment and a headless browser (Puppeteer/Chromium) for rendering; no network dependency but system setup is needed.
ev: GitHub repo (github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid-cli), PyPI package (pypi.org/project/mermaid-cli), x-cmd docs, and multiple community MCP server repos confirm functionality and integration patterns.

name: YouTube Transcript
what: REST API service providing YouTube video transcripts, search, channel, and playlist data via HTTP endpoints with API key auth. Primary vendor site is transcriptapi.com.
auth: API key (Bearer token in Authorization header)
access_note: Paid plans with credits; monthly/annual billing; all endpoints included in one plan. Sign up at transcriptapi.com.
